name: RENAL VEIN THROMBOSIS
definition: good collateral drainage; impaired function with steady state or recanalization indolent stage

demographics.Path: hemorrhagic renal infarction + edema from ruptured venules + capillaries without time
demographics.Location: more common on left (longer left renal vein)

dominant_finding: prolonged cortical nephrographic phase with coarse striations + persistent
distribution: more common on left (longer left renal vein)
discriminating_features: no characteristic pattern on sequential functional study
smooth enlargement of kidney ← edema + hemorrhage
range of completely normal to pyelocaliceal nonvisualization

general: smooth enlargement of kidney ← edema + hemorrhage
range of completely normal to pyelocaliceal nonvisualization
US: focal / generalized areas of increased echogenicity ← hemorrhage / edema
thrombus within distended renal vein / IVC
venous flow present in segmental veins + collateral veins overlying renal hilum
steady / less pulsatile venous flow compared with contralateral main renal vein
main renal vein not traceable into IVC on color Doppler
elevated resistive index > 0.70 ± reversed end-diastolic renal arterial flow in native
CT: prolonged cortical nephrographic phase with coarse striations + persistent
edema in renal sinus + perinephric space
attenuated renal vein ← retraction of blood clot + IVC thrombus (24%)
collaterals along proximal + middle ureter + perirenal
delayed / absent pyelocaliceal opacification + attenuated collecting system
enlarged venous collaterals on delayed images
MR: high signal intensity on T1WI + T2WI
ANGIO: absent inflow from renal vein into IVC
NUC: no characteristic pattern on sequential functional study
slightly diminished / normal nephrographic density (may increase over time)
collateral veins allow venous efflux → normalizing arterial waveform
main renal vein appears small ← recanalization
normal excretory urogram in 25% (with good collateral circulation especially if left side
notching of collecting system + proximal ureter
